name: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets

subControls.name: 1.1: Establish and Maintain Detailed Enterprise Asset Inventory
subControls.description: Create and regularly update a comprehensive inventory of all devices capable of storing or processing data, including PCs, mobile devices, servers, and IoT devices, across physical, virtual, and cloud environments.

subControls.name: 1.2: Address Unauthorized Assets
subControls.description: Implement a process to identify and remove unauthorized devices from the network, ensuring that only approved assets are connected.

subControls.name: 1.3: Utilize an Active Directory Tool
subControls.description: Employ an active discovery tool to detect devices connected to the enterprise network, scheduling scans daily or more frequently to maintain an up-to-date asset inventory.

subControls.name: 1.4: Use Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) Logging to Updat Enterprise Asset Inventory
subControls.description: Enable DHCP logging on all DHCP servers or IP address management tools to update the enterprise's asset inventory, reviewing logs weekly or more frequently.

subControls.name: 1.5: Use a Passive Asset Discovery Tool
subControls.description: Deploy a passive discovery tool to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network by monitoring network traffic, updating the asset inventory at least weekly.

name: Inventory and Control of Software Assets

subControls.name: 2.1: Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
subControls.description: Create and maintain an up-to-date inventory of all software installed on enterprise assets to ensure only authorized software is present.

subControls.name: 2.2: Ensure Authorized Software is Currently Supported
subControls.description: Ensure that only software currently supported by vendors is designated as authorized, removing or replacing unsupported software to reduce security risks.

subControls.name: 2.3: Address Unauthorized Software
subControls.description: Employ automated tools to track and manage software inventory, ensuring accuracy and completeness across all enterprise assets.

subControls.name: 2.4: Utilize Automated Software Inventory Tools
subControls.description: Monitor and record changes to the software inventory to detect unauthorized additions or modifications.

subControls.name: 2.5: Allowlist Authorized Software
subControls.description: Link software inventory data with hardware asset inventory to maintain a comprehensive view of enterprise assets.

subControls.name: 2.6: Allowlist Authorized Libraries
subControls.description: Identify and remove unauthorized software from enterprise assets, or document exceptions, to maintain control over the software environment.

subControls.name: 2.7: Allowlist Authorized Scripts
subControls.description: Implement application whitelisting to ensure that only approved software can execute on enterprise assets, preventing unauthorized applications from running.

subControls.name: 3.1: Establish and Maintain a Data Management Process
subControls.description: Define and manage processes for classifying, handling, storing, and disposing of data based on sensitivity and business needs.

subControls.name: 3.2: Establish and Maintain a Data Inventory
subControls.description: Maintain an inventory of all sensitive or regulated data types, including location, format, and owner.

subControls.name: 3.3: Configure Data Access Control Lists
subControls.description: Use access control lists to ensure only authorized users and systems can access specific datasets.

subControls.name: 3.4: Enforce Data Retention
subControls.description: Implement data retention policies to ensure data is only stored for as long as necessary for business or legal reasons.

subControls.name: 3.5: Securely Dispose of Data
subControls.description: Use secure deletion methods to permanently remove data when it is no longer needed.

subControls.name: 3.6: Encrypt data on End-User Devices
subControls.description: Ensure data on laptops, mobile devices, and other endpoints is encrypted to prevent unauthorized access.

subControls.name: 3.7: Establish and Maintain a Data Classification Scheme
subControls.description: Classify data based on sensitivity and importance, guiding how it is handled, stored, and protected.

subControls.name: 3.8: Document Data Flows
subControls.description: Identify and document how sensitive data moves across systems and networks to support risk assessment and protection.

subControls.name: 3.9: Encrypt Data on Removable Media
subControls.description: Use encryption to protect sensitive data stored on USB drives, external disks, and other removable media.

subControls.name: 3.10: Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit
subControls.description: Ensure that sensitive data is encrypted during transmission across public or untrusted networks.

subControls.name: 3.11: Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest
subControls.description: Use encryption to protect sensitive data stored on servers, databases, and other storage systems.

subControls.name: 3.12: Segment Data Processing and Storage Based on Sensitivity
subControls.description: Use network segmentation or isolated environments to separate processing of highly sensitive data from lower-risk data.

subControls.name: 3.13: Deploy a Data Loss Prevention Solution
subControls.description: Use DLP tools to detect and prevent unauthorized transmission or exposure of sensitive data.

subControls.name: 3.14: Log Sensitive Access
subControls.description: Maintain logs of when and by whom sensitive data is accessed to support audits and detect unauthorized use.

name: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software

subControls.name: 4.1: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
subControls.description: Develop and follow processes to configure systems securely and review settings regularly for compliance.

subControls.name: 4.2: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process for Network Infrastructure
subControls.description: Apply secure baseline configurations to routers, switches, firewalls, and other network devices, and review them periodically.

subControls.name: 4.3: Configure Automatic Session Locking on Enterprise Assets
subControls.description: Ensure that devices automatically lock sessions after a period of inactivity to reduce unauthorized access.

subControls.name: 4.4: Implement and Manage a Firewall on Servers
subControls.description: Install and configure host-based firewalls on servers to limit unwanted inbound and outbound traffic.

subControls.name: 4.5: Implement and Manage a Firewall on End-User Devices
subControls.description: Use local firewalls on laptops and workstations to help protect against unauthorized access.

subControls.name: 4.6: Securely Manage Enterprise Assets and Software
subControls.description: Ensure that all device management, including remote access and updates, is done securely using trusted protocols and tools.

subControls.name: 4.7: Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
subControls.description: Disable or rename default accounts and change default passwords to reduce the risk of exploitation.

subControls.name: 4.8: Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services on Enterprise Assets and Software
subControls.description: Remove or disable unused software and services to reduce the attack surface of systems.

subControls.name: 4.9: Configure Trusted DNS Servers on Enterprise Assets
subControls.description: Set devices to use only approved internal or secure external DNS servers for name resolution.

subControls.name: 4.10: Enforce Automatic Device Lockout on Portable End-User Devices
subControls.description: Ensure that mobile and portable devices are configured to automatically lock when idle or not in use.

subControls.name: 4.11: Enforce Remote Wipe Capability on Portable End-User Devices
subControls.description: Enable remote wipe features to delete data from lost or stolen mobile devices.

subControls.name: 4.12: Seperate Enterprise Workspaces on Mobile End-User Devices
subControls.description: Use containerization or separation tools to isolate enterprise apps and data from personal apps on mobile devices.

subControls.name: 5.1: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
subControls.description: Keep an accurate and up-to-date inventory of all user and service accounts across enterprise systems.

subControls.name: 5.2: Use Unique Passwords
subControls.description: Ensure that every account has a unique password to prevent credential reuse across systems.

subControls.name: 5.3: Disable Dormant Accounts
subControls.description: Regularly identify and disable accounts that have not been used within a defined time period.

subControls.name: 5.4: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
subControls.description: Separate admin rights from standard user accounts to limit exposure and improve oversight.

subControls.name: 5.5: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Accounts
subControls.description: Track and document service accounts used for system functions, including where and how they are used.

subControls.name: 5.6: Centralize Account Management
subControls.description: Manage account creation, modification, and removal centrally to improve security and oversight.

subControls.name: 6.1: Establish an Access Granting Process
subControls.description: Create a standardized process to approve and document user access based on role and need.

subControls.name: 6.2: Establish an Access Revoking Process
subControls.description: Ensure user access is promptly revoked when no longer needed, such as after termination or role change.

subControls.name: 6.3: Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
subControls.description: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all external access to applications and services.

subControls.name: 6.4: Require MFA for Remote Network Access
subControls.description: Use multi-factor authentication to protect remote access to internal networks and systems.

subControls.name: 6.5: Require MFA for Administrative Access
subControls.description: Enforce MFA for all administrative access to prevent unauthorized changes to systems.

subControls.name: 6.6: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Authentication and Authorization Systems
subControls.description: Document all systems used to manage user identities and access control across the organization.

subControls.name: 6.7: Centrilize Access Control
subControls.description: Use centralized access control systems to manage and enforce policies consistently across assets.

subControls.name: 6.8: Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
subControls.description: Assign user access based on roles and responsibilities to limit unnecessary permissions.

subControls.name: 7.1: Establish and Maintain a Vulnerability Management Process
subControls.description: Develop and follow a process for identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities across enterprise assets.

subControls.name: 7.2: Establish and Maintain a Remediation Process
subControls.description: Define timelines and responsibilities for remediating known vulnerabilities based on risk level.

subControls.name: 7.3: Perform Automated Operating System  Patch Management
subControls.description: Use automated tools to regularly apply security patches to operating systems.

subControls.name: 7.4: Perform Automated Application Patch Management
subControls.description: Ensure that applications receive timely security updates using automated patching tools.

subControls.name: 7.5: Perform Automated Vulnerability scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
subControls.description: Conduct regular internal vulnerability scans to identify and address security gaps in systems.

subControls.name: 7.6: Perform Automated Vulnerability scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
subControls.description: Scan internet-facing systems regularly to detect and remediate vulnerabilities before exploitation.

subControls.name: 7.7: Perform Automated Vulnerability scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
subControls.description: Fix or mitigate identified vulnerabilities based on severity and business risk.

subControls.name: 8.1: Establish and Maintain an Audit Log Management Process
subControls.description: Define how audit logs are collected, stored, reviewed, and retained across all relevant systems.

subControls.name: 8.2: Collect Audit Logs
subControls.description: Enable logging on all critical systems and applications to record security-relevant events.

subControls.name: 8.3: Ensure Adequate Audit Log Storage
subControls.description: Store logs in a secure location with enough capacity to meet retention and investigation needs.

subControls.name: 8.4: Standardize Time Synchronization
subControls.description: Use consistent time sources (e.g., NTP) across systems to ensure accurate log correlation.

subControls.name: 8.5: Collect Detailed Audit Logs
subControls.description: Capture detailed event information, including user actions, system changes, and authentication events.

subControls.name: 8.6: Collect DNS Query Audit Logs
subControls.description: Log DNS queries to help detect suspicious or unauthorized domain lookups.

subControls.name: 8.7: Collect URL Request Audit Logs
subControls.description: Record outbound web requests to monitor user activity and detect risky behavior.

subControls.name: 8.8: Collect Command-Line Audit Logs
subControls.description: Log command-line activity on servers and workstations to identify administrative or malicious actions.

subControls.name: 8.9: Centralize Audit Logs
subControls.description: Send logs to a central system for secure storage, analysis, and alerting.

subControls.name: 8.10: Retain Audit Logs
subControls.description: Keep logs for a defined period based on business, legal, and security requirements.

subControls.name: 8.11: Conduct Audit Log Reviews
subControls.description: Regularly review logs to detect anomalies, policy violations, or indicators of compromise.

subControls.name: 8.12: Collect Service Provider Logs
subControls.description: Ensure that third-party services generate and provide access to audit logs relevant to your environment.

subControls.name: 9.1: Ensure Use of Only Fully Supported Browser and Email Clients
subControls.description: Require users to use browsers and email clients that receive security updates from the vendor.

subControls.name: 9.2: Use DNS Filtering Services
subControls.description: Route DNS requests through filtering services to block access to malicious or unwanted domains.

subControls.name: 9.3: Maintain and Enforce Network-Based URL Filters
subControls.description: Use web filtering tools to restrict access to risky or non-business-related websites.

subControls.name: 9.4: Restrict Unnecessary or Unauthorized Browser and Email Client Extensions
subControls.description: Limit the use of extensions to only those approved and necessary for business.

subControls.name: 9.5: Implement DMARC
subControls.description: Deploy Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance to help prevent email spoofing.

subControls.name: 9.6: Block Unnecessary File Types
subControls.description: Prevent the download or delivery of file types commonly used in malware attacks, such as executables.

subControls.name: 9.7: Deploy and Maintain Email Server Anti-Malware Protections
subControls.description: Use anti-malware scanning on email servers to detect and block malicious attachments or links.

subControls.name: 10.1: Deploy and Maintain Anti-Malware Software
subControls.description: Install anti-malware solutions on all enterprise assets to detect and block known threats.

subControls.name: 10.2: Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Updates
subControls.description: Ensure malware protection tools automatically receive the latest signatures and threat intelligence.

subControls.name: 10.3: Disable Autorun and Autoplay for Removable Media
subControls.description: Turn off autorun/autoplay features to prevent malware from executing when devices are connected.

subControls.name: 10.4: Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Scanning of Removable Media
subControls.description: Configure systems to automatically scan USB drives and other removable media for malware.

subControls.name: 10.5: Enable Anti-Exploitation Features
subControls.description: Use features like Data Execution Prevention (DEP) and Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) to limit exploitability.

subControls.name: 10.6: Centrally Manage Anti-Malware Software
subControls.description: Control and monitor anti-malware settings and updates from a centralized management system.

subControls.name: 10.7: Use Behavior-Based Anti-Malware Software
subControls.description: Employ tools that analyze behavior patterns to detect new or unknown malware.

subControls.name: 11.1: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
subControls.description: Develop and document procedures to recover data after an incident such as deletion, corruption, or ransomware.

subControls.name: 11.2: Perform Automated Backups
subControls.description: Schedule automated backups of critical data to ensure recovery in case of data loss.

subControls.name: 11.3: Protect Recovery Data
subControls.description: Secure backup data with encryption and access controls to prevent unauthorized access or tampering.

subControls.name: 11.4: Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data
subControls.description: Store backups separately from the primary environment to protect against ransomware and other threats.

subControls.name: 11.5: Test Data Recovery
subControls.description: Regularly test your ability to restore systems and data to validate that recovery processes work as expected.

subControls.name: 12.1: Ensure Network Infrastructure is Up-to-Date
subControls.description: Keep all network devices updated with supported firmware and software versions to reduce vulnerabilities.

subControls.name: 12.2: Establish and Maintain a Secure Network Architecture
subControls.description: Design networks with segmentation and secure zones to limit the spread of attacks.

subControls.name: 12.3: Securely Manage Network Infrastructure
subControls.description: Use encrypted management protocols and restrict access to configuration interfaces.

subControls.name: 12.4:Establish and Maintain Architecture Diagram(s)
subControls.description: Maintain accurate network diagrams to support troubleshooting, audits, and risk assessments.

subControls.name: 12.5: Centrilize Network Authentication, Authorization, and Auditing (AAA)
subControls.description: Control access to network devices using centralized AAA services to improve visibility and control.

subControls.name: 12.6: Use of Secure Network Management and Communication Protocols
subControls.description: Enforce encrypted protocols like SSH, SNMPv3, and HTTPS for managing network equipment.

subControls.name: 12.7: Ensure Remote Devices Utilize a VPN and are connecting to an Enterprise's AAA Infrastructure
subControls.description: Ensure remote users access the network only via a secure VPN that enforces enterprise access policies.

subControls.name: 12.8: Establish and Maintain Dedicated Computing Resources for All Administrative Work
subControls.description: Use dedicated and hardened workstations for administrative tasks to reduce exposure to threats.

subControls.name: 13.1: Centrilize Security Event Alerting
subControls.description: Send security alerts from all systems to a central platform for monitoring and response.

subControls.name: 13.2: Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Detection Solution
subControls.description: Install intrusion detection tools on endpoints to detect suspicious activity.

subControls.name: 13.3: Deploy a Network Intrusion Detection System
subControls.description: Use NIDS to monitor network traffic for signs of malicious behavior or policy violations.

subControls.name: 13.4: Perform Traffic Filtering Between Network Segments
subControls.description: Control and monitor traffic between different parts of the network using firewalls or segmentation.

subControls.name: 13.5: Manage Access Control for Remote Assets
subControls.description: Enforce access controls and policies for devices connecting from outside the enterprise network.

subControls.name: 13.6: Collect Network Trafik Flow Logs
subControls.description: Log metadata about network traffic (e.g., source/destination, ports, protocol) for analysis.

subControls.name: 13.7: Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Prevention Solution
subControls.description: Use HIPS to detect and block attacks in real time on individual systems.

subControls.name: 13.8: Deploy a Network Intrusion Prevention System
subControls.description: Use NIPS to automatically block known threats detected in network traffic.

subControls.name: 13.9: Deploy Port-Level Access Control
subControls.description: Restrict network access at the switch port level using technologies like 802.1X.

subControls.name: 13.10: Perform Application Layer Filtering
subControls.description: Filter traffic based on application protocols (e.g., HTTP, DNS) to block risky behaviors.

subControls.name: 13.11: Tune Security Event Alerting Tresholds
subControls.description: Adjust alert settings to reduce noise and prioritize meaningful security events..

subControls.name: 14.1: Establish and Maintain a Security Awareness Program
subControls.description: Create a program to train users regularly on security risks, behaviors, and policies.

subControls.name: 14.2: Train Workforce Members to Recognize Social Engineering Attacks
subControls.description: Teach users to identify and report phishing, pretexting, and other social engineering tactics.

subControls.name: 14.3: Train Workforce Members on Authentication Best Practices
subControls.description: Educate users on creating strong passwords, using MFA, and managing credentials securely.

subControls.name: 14.4: Train Workforce on Best Practices for Data Handling
subControls.description: Instruct employees on how to classify, store, and transmit data securely.

subControls.name: 14.5: Train Workforce Members on Causes of Unintentional Data Exposure
subControls.description: Raise awareness of risky behaviors like misconfigurations or accidental sharing.

subControls.name: 14.6: Train Workforce Members on Recognizing and Reporting Security Incidents
subControls.description: Ensure all users know how and where to report suspected security issues.

subControls.name: 14.7: Train Users on How to Identify and Report if their Enterprise Assets are Missing Security Updates
subControls.description: Help users recognize when devices are missing patches or updates and how to respond.

subControls.name: 14.8: Train Workforce on the Dangers of Connecting to and Transmitting Enterprise Data Over Insecure Networks
subControls.description: Warn users against connecting to untrusted Wi-Fi and teach safe alternatives like VPNs

subControls.name: 14.9: Conduct Role-Specific Security Awareness and Skills Training
subControls.description: Provide tailored security training based on an employee’s role and access level.

subControls.name: 15.1: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Providers
subControls.description: Track all third-party providers that process, store, or transmit organizational data.

subControls.name: 15.2: Establish and Maintain a Service Provider Management Policy
subControls.description: Document requirements for evaluating, onboarding, and managing external service providers.

subControls.name: 15.3: Classify Service Providers
subControls.description: Categorize providers based on the sensitivity of the data or services they handle.

subControls.name: 15.4: Ensure Service Provider Contracts Include Security Requirements
subControls.description: Include data protection, incident reporting, and audit rights in service provider agreements.

subControls.name: 15.5: Assess Service Providers
subControls.description: Periodically evaluate providers’ security posture through assessments or audits.

subControls.name: 15.6: Monitor Service Providers
subControls.description: Regularly review provider performance and security practices to ensure compliance.

subControls.name: 15.7: Securely Decommission Service Providers
subControls.description: Revoke access and ensure secure data removal when terminating provider relationships.

subControls.name: 16.1: Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process
subControls.description: Define secure coding and testing practices to follow throughout the development lifecycle.

subControls.name: 16.2: Establish and Maintain a Process to Accept and Address Software Vulnerabilities
subControls.description: Create a process to receive, track, and remediate reported security flaws in applications.

subControls.name: 16.3: Perform Root Cause Analysis on Security Vulnerabilities
subControls.description: Analyze underlying issues in code or processes to prevent repeated vulnerabilities.

subControls.name: 16.4: Establish and Manage an Inventory of Third-Party Software Components
subControls.description: Maintain a list of external libraries and packages used in your applications.

subControls.name: 16.5: Use Up-to-Date and Trusted Third-Party Software Components
subControls.description: Ensure third-party components are vetted and kept up to date with security patches.

subControls.name: 16.6: Establish and Maintain a Severity Rating System and Process for Application Vulnerabilities
subControls.description: Prioritize fixes using a formal rating system based on risk and potential impact.

subControls.name: 16.7: Use Standard Hardening Configuration Templates for Application Infrastructure
subControls.description: Apply standard hardening templates to systems supporting application infrastructure.

subControls.name: 16.8: Separate Production and Non-Production Systems
subControls.description: Isolate dev/test environments from production to prevent accidental or malicious access.

subControls.name: 16.9: Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding
subControls.description: Ensure developers understand secure coding practices and common vulnerabilities.

subControls.name: 16.10: Apply Secure Design Principles in Application Architectures
subControls.description: Incorporate security at the architectural level during software design.

subControls.name: 16.11: Leverage Vetted Modules or Services for Application Security Components
subControls.description: Implement proven, tested modules for key security functions like authentication and encryption.

subControls.name: 16.12: Implement Code-Level Security Checks
subControls.description: Use automated tools to scan source code for flaws before deployment.

subControls.name: 16.13: Conduct Application Penetration Testing
subControls.description: Regularly test applications using ethical hacking techniques to identify weaknesses.

subControls.name: 16.14: Conduct Threat Modeling
subControls.description: Analyze potential threats to your applications to guide design and mitigation.

subControls.name: 17.1: Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling
subControls.description: Assign staff responsible for managing and coordinating incident response efforts.

subControls.name: 17.2: Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents
subControls.description: Keep up-to-date contact info for reporting and escalating security incidents.

subControls.name: 17.3: Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents
subControls.description: Create clear procedures for reporting security incidents internally and externally.

subControls.name: 17.4: Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process
subControls.description: Document steps for detecting, responding to, and recovering from incidents.

subControls.name: 17.5: Assign Key Roles and Responsibilities
subControls.description: Define roles in the incident response process, including decision-makers and communicators.

subControls.name: 17.6: Define Mechanisms for Communicating During Incident Response
subControls.description: Use secure and reliable methods to coordinate during security incidents.

subControls.name: 17.7: Conduct Routine Incident Response Exercises
subControls.description: Test the organization’s ability to respond to incidents through regular simulations.

subControls.name: 17.8: Conduct Post-Incident Reviews
subControls.description: After an incident, review what happened and improve the response process accordingly.

subControls.name: 17.9: Establish and Maintain Security Incident Thresholds
subControls.description: Establish what qualifies as a security incident to ensure appropriate response.

subControls.name: 18.1: Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program
subControls.description: Define how and when penetration testing should be performed to evaluate defenses.

subControls.name: 18.2: Perform Periodic External Penetration Tests
subControls.description: Test internet-facing systems for exploitable vulnerabilities at regular intervals.

subControls.name: 18.3: Remediate Penetration Test Findings
subControls.description: Fix or mitigate issues identified during penetration tests based on severity.

subControls.name: 18.4: Validate Security Measures
subControls.description: Use testing to confirm that security controls are effective and working as intended.

subControls.name: 18.5: Perform Periodic Internal Penetration Tests
subControls.description: Simulate attacks from inside the network to assess lateral movement and internal risks.
